content: Curiosity-driven autonomous agents can integrate intrinsic ICM/RND curiosity signals into a hybrid planning and learning framework to dynamically split exploration between deliberative long-term planning and reactive reward-seeking learning, where curiosity prioritizes planning for high-uncertainty regions of environment space the agent has not encoded in its memory. This mechanism lets the agent avoid redundant exploratory sampling of already learned areas while proactively targeting under-explored zones that deliver long-term knowledge gains, rather than just reacting to random novelty.
